sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. אָסַף to scrape, to scrape together (see Hiphil No. 1), to scrape off, Isa 7:20 to take away life, Psa 40:15 to take any one away, to destroy, Gen 18:23, 24 Gen 18:24and intrans. to be destroyed, to perish, Jer 12:4.

(2) i.q. יָסַף (but אָסַף and יָסַף are of the same stock; see page 67, A .), to add, only in imp. סְפוּ Isa 29:1 Jer 7:21 and inf. סְפֹות Isa 30:1. [“But these may be from יָסַף.”] Also, to add to anything, to increase (see יָסַף No. 2 ), Num 32:14.

Niphal-

(1) i.q. נֶאֱסַף No. 2, to betake oneself (into the house). Isa 13:15, כָּל־הַנִּסְפֶּה “whoever betakes (or, withdraws) himself;” i.e. lurks in houses, hides away. Opp. to כָּל־הַנִּמְצָא.

(2) to be taken away, to perish, Gen 19:15 especially in battle, 1Sa 12:25 26:10 27:1 .

Hiphil, to heap together, to accumulate; followed by עַל upon any one, Deu 32:23. LXX. συνάξω.
